text: The Bachelor of Nursing Science prepares students for professional nursing practice through hands-on clinical experience. Graduate programs include nurse practitioner, research-based, and online doctoral pathways.
Outdoor and Experiential Education introduces teacher candidates to environmental and experiential learning approaches. Graduates are eligible for Ontario College of Teachers certification.
Philosophy develops critical thinking and analytical skills through the study of ethics, political philosophy, metaphysics, epistemology, and philosophy of science.
Physics and Astronomy combines undergraduate teaching with high-calibre research, offering opportunities to work on interdisciplinary and international research projects.
Political Studies examines power, governance, political institutions, and public policy. The program emphasizes political behaviour, social diversity, and global inequality.
Politics, Philosophy and Economics is a multidisciplinary program that integrates analytical approaches from economics, political science, and philosophy to address complex societal challenges.
Primary-Junior French as a Second Language Education prepares future educators for bilingual teaching roles. Programs are offered in both on-campus and blended formats, leading to OCT certification.

metadata.program_name: Primary-Junior French as a Second Language Education

text: The Principal’s Qualification Program is an Ontario post-graduate certificate designed for experienced educators seeking leadership roles. Courses are accredited by the Ontario College of Teachers and delivered online.
The Professional Editing Standards Certificate prepares learners for industry-standard editorial practice and professional certification through foundational training in editing disciplines.

The Certificate in Professional Impact Analysis teaches methods for measuring and reporting social, environmental, and economic impact. The program is designed for professionals in public and private sectors.

Psychology studies mental processes and behaviour, exploring cognition, neuroscience, social influence, development, and mental health. Students benefit from hands-on research opportunities across multiple labs.

The Reading and Literacy Studies in Education program focuses on developing advanced expertise in literacy instruction, assessment, and curriculum design. This Ontario Post-Graduate Certificate supports educators in enhancing reading and literacy outcomes and can be laddered into graduate education programs.

metadata.program_name: Reading and Literacy Studies in Education

text: Religious Studies at Queen’s explores religious traditions from multicultural, diverse, and interdisciplinary perspectives. Students examine the historical, social, political, and cultural roles of religion, as well as secularism, belief systems, and religious practices across societies.

The Sexual and Gender Diversity Certificate Program provides an interdisciplinary framework for studying sexuality, gender, and queer/trans issues. Students engage with topics across disciplines such as health, law, politics, religion, music, and social sciences.

Sociology examines how societies function at multiple levels, from interpersonal relationships to global systems. The program emphasizes critical analysis of power, inequality, social justice, criminology, law, media, and surveillance.

Software Design prepares students to push the boundaries of computer systems through advanced training in software architecture, analysis, and evolution. Graduates pursue careers in software development, game design, entrepreneurship, and digital innovation.

Spanish and Latin American Studies offers interdisciplinary study of language, literature, culture, history, politics, and economics of Latin America. The program includes advanced language acquisition and flexible course pathways that enhance global and career perspectives.

The Special Education Post-Graduate Certificate equips educators with advanced knowledge and practical strategies for supporting diverse learners. Accredited by the Ontario College of Teachers, it can be laddered into graduate education programs.

Statistics focuses on designing experiments, analyzing data, and drawing valid conclusions. The program provides strong quantitative foundations applicable to science, engineering, medicine, government, and industry.

The Teacher Leadership Post-Graduate Certificate develops leadership capacity among educators through research-based practices aligned with Ontario Ministry of Education policies. Credits may be laddered into graduate education pathways.

Teaching English as a Second Language (TESL) prepares educators to teach English to learners worldwide. TESL 1 and TESL 2 certificates combine online coursework with practical teaching experience and practicum components.

metadata.program_name: Teaching English as a Second Language

text: The Teaching English Language Learners Post-Graduate Certificate provides educators with specialized skills to support multilingual learners. The program aligns with Ontario education standards and can be laddered into advanced graduate credentials.

The Teaching Mathematics Post-Graduate Certificate enhances educators’ expertise in mathematics instruction, assessment, and curriculum. The program is accredited by the Ontario College of Teachers and supports graduate-level advancement.

Technological Education prepares students with industry experience to teach Grades 9–12 in broad technological fields such as communications, computer technology, health care, and green industries. Offered in on-campus and blended formats.

The Technology in Teaching Post-Graduate Certificate focuses on integrating digital tools and computing technologies into educational practice. The program aligns with Ontario educational standards and supports career advancement.

Urban Planning Studies examines land use, governance, environmental policy, and social equity in urban and rural contexts. The certificate emphasizes interdisciplinary approaches to addressing complex planning challenges.

Black Studies is an interdisciplinary program exploring connections between the arts, social justice, decolonial thought, and anti-oppression. The curriculum spans multiple disciplines including history, literature, philosophy, global health, and political thought.
The Certificate in Business allows students pursuing non-business degrees to gain foundational knowledge in business management, expanding career opportunities and professional skills.
